html:
Exclusive to:
This content is available only after activating the Lunar Rift

“"Well, A Rather Beautifully Intelligent System." Uses a complicated assortment of cogs and circuitry for computing.
Once a target has been selected, as long as it stays in range, the W.A.R.B.I.S. begins calculating optimal performance to fight the target.
The armor improves the wearer's speed as it analyzes the target, ending at a 30% speed boost.
When combined with the W.A.R.B.I.S. headgear, the two will sync up for a few moments sharing target information.”

-

The W.A.R.B.I.S. Armor is a craftable Armor exclusive to Don't Starve Together. It requires 8 Scrap, 3 Electrical Doodads, and 1 Enlightened Shard to craft after learning the rare Blueprint that has a chance to drop from hammering Abandoned Junk.

The W.A.R.B.I.S. Armor has a Durability of 730 (over twice as durable as a Log Suit), and absorbs 85% of all physical damage and a flat 5 Planar Damage. It also insulates the player against Lightning strikes, turns all Electric Damage into Physical Damage, and ignore Shock effect.

Wearing and fighting with it analyzes a single target and increases the walking speed of the wearer over time up to a maximum of a +30% speed boost after a period of 20 seconds. This bonus is reset after either attacking a different target, or walking too far away (4 Tiles) from the current target. Unlike the W.A.R.B.I.S. Head Gear, the Dramatically Updating Diagram (D.U.D.) does not show up.

Analysis
Stage Time to reach Speed Boost
0 Immediate +10%
1 10 seconds +15%
2 16 seconds +20%
3 20 seconds +30%

The W.A.R.B.I.S. Armor can be combined with the W.A.R.B.I.S. Head Gear to get a damage boost in addition to the speed. The Head Gear will get synced with the current stage of the Armor within a few seconds, shown through waves at the top of the diagram, so the damage boost can be obtained without an additional analysis. When taking off the Armor or the Head Gear, the current state of the Analysis remains as long as the other is worn.

When the W.A.R.B.I.S. Armor's durability reaches 0%, it does not disappear; instead, it is unequipped and cannot be equipped until repaired. It can be fully repaired by one Auto-Mat-O-Chanic.

Contents

  • 1 Skill Tree (WX-78)
    • 1.1 Beta Circuits Tinkering I
  • 2 Trivia
  • 3 Gallery

Skill Tree (WX-78)

Beta Circuits Tinkering I

WX-78 with "Beta Circuits Tinkering I" skill and Rangebooster Circuits can increase the range of W.A.R.B.I.S target locking. Each increase 6 Wall units (1.5 Tiles).

Trivia

  • W.A.R.B.I.S. Armor was introduced in the Host of Horrors update.

Gallery

  • Dropped W.A.R.B.I.S. Armor
  • Dropped Broken W.A.R.B.I.S. Armor
  • W.A.R.B.I.S. Armor concept animation from Rhymes with Play
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

DSTDSRoGSWHam

Exclusive to:

“Fearsome and gearsome.”
–Wilson
“It looks pretty fireproof, but there's only one way to know for sure...”
–Willow
“Wolfgang not need robot armor for protection, only like it for color.”
–Wolfgang
“Even the thickest metal couldn't protect my heart.”
–Wendy
“THIS POWER SUIT IS A MUST-HAVE FOR ANY DEADLY OCCASION”
–WX-78
“I do wish it wasn't quite so noisy.”
–Wickerbottom
“Does it come in plaid?”
–Woodie
“What it lacks in style it almost makes up for in pure, stubborn resilience.”
–Maxwell
“It grants the wearer a fleetness öf fööt tö rival Hermöd himself.”
–Wigfrid
“Ms. Wickerbottom said to keep our fingers away from the gears.”
–Webber
“A hard shell to protect my soft, delicious insides.”
–Warly
“Be strong like robot friend!”
–Wormwood
“Well ain't that a fancy lil' robo-romper.”
–Winona
“Don't leer at my gear.”
–Wortox
“Ooh, Ironfolk shirt.”
–Wurt
“I'll be part boy, part machine!”
–Walter
“You can always rely on clockwork.”
–Wanda
